properties.name: Koshi River
properties.note: Main trunk of the Koshi system downstream of the Arun–Tamor–Sunkoshi confluence.

properties.name: Arun River
properties.note: Largest tributary contribution in the WECS gross-potential breakdown for Koshi.

properties.name: Tamor River
properties.note: Major eastern Koshi tributary with strong gross potential.

properties.name: Sun Koshi River
properties.note: Core central-eastern tributary corridor feeding the Koshi system.

properties.name: Dudh Koshi River
properties.note: Important tributary and storage corridor with major project interest.

properties.name: Tama Koshi River
properties.note: High-value hydropower corridor including Upper Tamakoshi.

properties.name: Bhote Koshi River
properties.note: Steep trans-Himalayan tributary with dense existing hydropower development.

properties.name: Indrawati River
properties.note: Important eastern-central tributary and part of the broader Sunkoshi corridor.

properties.name: Likhu Khola
properties.note: Smaller but hydropower-relevant Koshi tributary.

properties.name: Balephi Khola
properties.note: Hydropower-relevant tributary within the Sunkoshi sub-system.

properties.name: Narayani River
properties.note: Main trunk of the Gandaki system in the plains and middle hills.

properties.name: Kali Gandaki River
properties.note: Largest gross-potential tributary within the Gandaki basin in WECS 2019.

properties.name: Marsyangdi River
properties.note: Dense existing hydropower corridor in central Nepal.

properties.name: Budhi Gandaki River
properties.note: Major tributary and proposed large storage corridor.

properties.name: Trishuli River
properties.note: Central corridor tributary with major generation and transmission relevance.

properties.name: Seti Gandaki River
properties.note: Western-central Gandaki tributary with storage and peaking relevance.

properties.name: Madi River
properties.note: Secondary but still relevant tributary in the Gandaki system.

properties.name: East Rapti River
properties.note: Gandaki-connected Rapti tributary with hydropower and floodplain relevance.

properties.name: Karnali River
properties.note: Main western Himalayan trunk and one of Nepal's most strategic undeveloped power systems.

properties.name: Bheri River
properties.note: Largest gross-potential tributary in the Karnali system according to WECS.

properties.name: Humla Karnali River
properties.note: Upper western Himalayan tributary with high head and strong strategic potential.

properties.name: Mugu Karnali River
properties.note: Upper western tributary relevant to long-term storage and peaking options.

properties.name: Tila River
properties.note: Mid-sized Karnali tributary with ongoing project interest.

properties.name: Mahakali River
properties.note: Western border river and site of the long-discussed Pancheshwar multipurpose project.

properties.name: Babai River
properties.note: Medium basin with more development-facing than export-facing significance.

properties.name: Bagmati River
properties.note: Central medium basin with high urban and environmental relevance.

properties.name: Kamala River
properties.note: Eastern medium basin with strong seasonal characteristics.

properties.name: Kankai River
properties.note: Eastern medium basin with hydropower value but limited large-system significance.

properties.name: Tinau River
properties.note: Smaller basin included in the WECS nationwide potential screening.

properties.name: Mechi River
properties.note: Eastern border basin with limited gross hydropower potential in the national total.
